description: Fill the remaining E2E test gaps for dev proxy hot reload: multi-client end-to-end tool dispatch after reload, rapid successive reloads during SSE streams, and side panel UI state after hot reload (process restart).

userStories.title: E2E test: multiple concurrent MCP clients dispatch tools end-to-end after hot reload
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an E2E test that verifies multiple concurrent MCP clients can all successfully dispatch tools through the extension after a dev proxy hot reload, so that I can be confident multi-client setups survive hot reloads without silent failures.
acceptanceCriteria: A new test in e2e/dev-proxy.e2e.ts (or hot-reload-dynamic.e2e.ts) creates 2 MCP clients and 1 extension context
Both MCP clients call a plugin tool (e.g., e2e-test_echo) through the extension before the hot reload and verify the result
A hot reload is triggered via triggerHotReload()
After the hot reload completes and the extension reconnects, both MCP clients call e2e-test_echo again and verify correct results
The test asserts both clients get correct tool output (not just listTools()) post-reload
userStories.notes:
The existing tests 'all MCP sessions see updated tools after hot reload' and 'all sessions see config changes after hot reload' in e2e/hot-reload-dynamic.e2e.ts (lines 437 and 467) only call listTools() after hot reload — they never actually dispatch a tool through the extension for both clients. The gap is that listTools() is a server-side operation that doesn't exercise the extension WebSocket relay at all.

Add the test to e2e/dev-proxy.e2e.ts in a new describe block like 'Dev proxy multi-client tool dispatch after hot reload'. Use the full fixture set (mcpServer, testServer, extensionContext, mcpClient) plus create a second MCP client manually using createMcpClient(). Follow the pattern in e2e/hot-reload-dynamic.e2e.ts:499 ('plugin added via config is callable from pre-existing MCP session after hot reload') which already tests single-client end-to-end dispatch after reload.

Key steps:
1. Use the standard fixtures for one client; create a second client with createMcpClient()
2. Set up tool test page, wait for adapter injection
3. Both clients call e2e-test_echo (baseline)
4. triggerHotReload()
5. Wait for 'Hot reload complete' log and extension reconnect (waitForLog + waitForExtensionConnected)
6. Both clients call e2e-test_echo again and assert correct output

The second MCP client needs re-initialization after hot reload (the worker process is replaced). Use the same retry-on-502/503 pattern that the fixture mcpClient uses — see e2e/fixtures.ts createMcpClient() and the MCP client's initialize() method which handles 502/503 responses.

userStories.title: E2E test: rapid successive hot reloads during active SSE stream
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an E2E test that fires 2-3 rapid hot reloads while an MCP client has an active SSE tool call in progress, so that I can verify the proxy handles overlapping restart+SSE scenarios without deadlocks or orphaned connections.
acceptanceCriteria: A new test in e2e/dev-proxy.e2e.ts fires a slow tool call (e.g., slow_with_progress) that produces SSE progress events
While the SSE stream is active, 2-3 triggerHotReload() calls are fired in rapid succession (not awaited between them)
The slow tool call either completes, errors cleanly, or times out — but never hangs indefinitely
After all hot reloads settle, the MCP client can re-initialize and successfully call a tool (e.g., e2e-test_echo) end-to-end through the extension
No deadlock, state corruption, or uncaught exception appears in server logs
userStories.notes:
The existing test 'SSE tool call gets clean error or completes when worker restarts mid-stream' in e2e/dev-proxy.e2e.ts (line 467) fires only ONE hot reload during the SSE stream. The gap is testing what happens with 2-3 rapid reloads overlapping the SSE stream — the proxy must handle multiple worker restarts while the upstream SSE connection is being torn down and rebuilt.

Add this test in the existing 'Dev proxy SSE mid-stream worker restart' describe block in e2e/dev-proxy.e2e.ts. Follow the same setup pattern as the existing test (lines 467-542):
1. Full fixture set (mcpServer, testServer, extensionContext, mcpClient)
2. Set up tool test page, wait for adapter injection
3. Baseline tool call to confirm everything works
4. Start a slow_with_progress call (5s duration, produces progress events)
5. Wait ~500ms for the SSE stream to start flowing
6. Fire 3 triggerHotReload() calls in rapid succession (no await between them)
7. Await the slow call promise — success or error both acceptable
8. Wait for 'Hot reload complete' log
9. Wait for extension reconnect (waitForExtensionConnected)
10. Call e2e-test_echo and verify correct output
11. Assert no ECONNREFUSED/deadlock/state corruption/uncaughtException in logs

The e2e-test plugin's slow_with_progress tool is in plugins/e2e-test — it takes a duration param and emits progress notifications via SSE. The MCP client in fixtures.ts supports SSE streams via the callTool() method.

userStories.title: E2E test: side panel UI shows connected state after dev proxy hot reload
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an E2E test that opens the side panel, triggers a dev proxy hot reload (process restart), and verifies the side panel DOM shows the correct connected state and plugin list after the reload completes, so that I can be confident the side panel recovers from process-level restarts.
acceptanceCriteria: A new test in e2e/side-panel-reload.e2e.ts (or a new file) opens the side panel, verifies it shows the plugin list, triggers a hot reload via triggerHotReload(), and verifies the side panel still shows the plugin list after reload
The test verifies the side panel shows the e2e-test plugin name ('E2E Test') before AND after the hot reload
The test waits for 'Hot reload complete' and extension reconnect before checking the post-reload state
userStories.notes:
Existing side panel tests in e2e/side-panel-reload.e2e.ts test POST /reload (in-process config rediscovery) but never test dev proxy hot reload (process restart via triggerHotReload). The side-panel-live-update.e2e.ts tests config.json changes with the config watcher. Neither exercises the full worker-kill-and-restart path that the dev proxy performs.

Add this test to e2e/side-panel-reload.e2e.ts in a new describe block like 'Side panel recovery after dev proxy hot reload'. Use a manual setup (not the standard fixture, since side panel tests need custom setup):

1. Create a config dir with writeTestConfig() including the e2e-test plugin
2. Start MCP server with hot=true via startMcpServer()
3. Launch extension context via launchExtensionContext()
4. Set up adapter symlink via setupAdapterSymlink()
5. Wait for extension connected via waitForExtensionConnected()
6. Open side panel via openSidePanel() helper from e2e/helpers.ts
7. Assert side panel shows 'E2E Test' plugin (locator 'text=E2E Test')
8. server.triggerHotReload()
9. Wait for 'Hot reload complete' log
10. Wait for extension reconnect via waitForExtensionConnected()
11. Assert side panel still shows 'E2E Test' plugin — may need to wait for the sync.full → side panel re-render pipeline to complete (use Playwright's toBeVisible with timeout)
12. Clean up: close context, kill server, rm temp dirs

The side panel re-render is driven by: worker restart → new sync.full to extension → chrome.storage update → offscreen broadcasts ws:message → side panel App.tsx detects sync.full and re-renders. This pipeline takes a few seconds, so use a generous timeout (30s) for the post-reload assertion.

Follow the exact cleanup pattern from the existing tests in e2e/side-panel-reload.e2e.ts (the try/finally with context.close(), server.kill(), fs.rmSync, cleanupTestConfigDir).
